speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: If you go on thus, you will kill yourself,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: And ’tis not wisdom thus to second grief

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Against yourself.

speaker: LEONATO
text: I pray thee, cease thy counsel,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Which falls into mine ears as profitless

speaker: LEONATO
text: As water in a sieve. Give not me counsel,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Nor let no comforter delight mine ear

speaker: LEONATO
text: But such a one whose wrongs do suit with mine.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Bring me a father that so loved his child,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Whose joy of her is overwhelmed like mine,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And bid him speak of patience.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Measure his woe the length and breadth of mine,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And let it answer every strain for strain,

speaker: LEONATO
text: As thus for thus, and such a grief for such,

speaker: LEONATO
text: In every lineament, branch, shape, and form.

speaker: LEONATO
text: If such a one will smile and stroke his beard,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Bid sorrow wag, cry “hem” when he should groan,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Patch grief with proverbs, make misfortune drunk

speaker: LEONATO
text: With candle-wasters, bring him yet to me,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And I of him will gather patience.

speaker: LEONATO
text: But there is no such man. For, brother, men

speaker: LEONATO
text: Can counsel and speak comfort to that grief

speaker: LEONATO
text: Which they themselves not feel, but tasting it,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Their counsel turns to passion, which before

speaker: LEONATO
text: Would give preceptial med’cine to rage,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Fetter strong madness in a silken thread,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Charm ache with air and agony with words.

speaker: LEONATO
text: No, no, ’tis all men’s office to speak patience

speaker: LEONATO
text: To those that wring under the load of sorrow,

speaker: LEONATO
text: But no man’s virtue nor sufficiency

speaker: LEONATO
text: To be so moral when he shall endure

speaker: LEONATO
text: The like himself. Therefore give me no counsel.

speaker: LEONATO
text: My griefs cry louder than advertisement.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Therein do men from children nothing differ.

speaker: LEONATO
text: I pray thee, peace. I will be flesh and blood,

speaker: LEONATO
text: For there was never yet philosopher

speaker: LEONATO
text: That could endure the toothache patiently,

speaker: LEONATO
text: However they have writ the style of gods

speaker: LEONATO
text: And made a push at chance and sufferance.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Yet bend not all the harm upon yourself.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Make those that do offend you suffer too.

speaker: LEONATO
text: There thou speak’st reason. Nay, I will do so.

speaker: LEONATO
text: My soul doth tell me Hero is belied,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And that shall Claudio know; so shall the Prince

speaker: LEONATO
text: And all of them that thus dishonor her.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Here comes the Prince and Claudio hastily.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Good e’en, good e’en.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Good day to both of you.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Hear you, my lords—

speaker: PRINCE
text: We have some haste, Leonato.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Some haste, my lord! Well, fare you well, my lord.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Are you so hasty now? Well, all is one.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Nay, do not quarrel with us, good old man.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: If he could right himself with quarrelling,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Some of us would lie low.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Who wrongs him?

speaker: LEONATO
text: Marry, thou dost wrong me, thou dissembler, thou.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Nay, never lay thy hand upon thy sword.

speaker: LEONATO
text: I fear thee not.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Marry, beshrew my hand

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: If it should give your age such cause of fear.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: In faith, my hand meant nothing to my sword.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Tush, tush, man, never fleer and jest at me.

speaker: LEONATO
text: I speak not like a dotard nor a fool,

speaker: LEONATO
text: As under privilege of age to brag

speaker: LEONATO
text: What I have done being young, or what would do

speaker: LEONATO
text: Were I not old. Know, Claudio, to thy head,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Thou hast so wronged mine innocent child and me

speaker: LEONATO
text: That I am forced to lay my reverence by,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And with gray hairs and bruise of many days

speaker: LEONATO
text: Do challenge thee to trial of a man.

speaker: LEONATO
text: I say thou hast belied mine innocent child.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Thy slander hath gone through and through her heart,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And she lies buried with her ancestors,

speaker: LEONATO
text: O, in a tomb where never scandal slept,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Save this of hers, framed by thy villainy.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: My villainy?

speaker: LEONATO
text: Thine, Claudio, thine, I say.

speaker: PRINCE
text: You say not right, old man.

speaker: LEONATO
text: My lord, my lord,

speaker: LEONATO
text: I’ll prove it on his body if he dare,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Despite his nice fence and his active practice,

speaker: LEONATO
text: His May of youth and bloom of lustihood.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Away! I will not have to do with you.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Canst thou so daff me? Thou hast killed my child.

speaker: LEONATO
text: If thou kill’st me, boy, thou shalt kill a man.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: He shall kill two of us, and men indeed,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: But that’s no matter. Let him kill one first.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Win me and wear me! Let him answer me.—

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Come, follow me, boy. Come, sir boy, come, follow me.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Sir boy, I’ll whip you from your foining fence,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Nay, as I am a gentleman, I will.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Brother—

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Content yourself. God knows I loved my niece,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: And she is dead, slandered to death by villains

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: That dare as well answer a man indeed

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: As I dare take a serpent by the tongue.—

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Boys, apes, braggarts, jacks, milksops!

speaker: LEONATO
text: Brother Anthony—

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Hold you content. What, man! I know them, yea,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: And what they weigh, even to the utmost scruple—

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Scambling, outfacing, fashionmonging boys,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: That lie and cog and flout, deprave and slander,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Go anticly and show outward hideousness,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: And speak off half a dozen dang’rous words

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: How they might hurt their enemies, if they durst,

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: And this is all.

speaker: LEONATO
text: But brother Anthony—

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Come, ’tis no matter.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Do not you meddle. Let me deal in this.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Gentlemen both, we will not wake your patience.

speaker: PRINCE
text: My heart is sorry for your daughter’s death,

speaker: PRINCE
text: But, on my honor, she was charged with nothing

speaker: PRINCE
text: But what was true and very full of proof.

speaker: LEONATO
text: My lord, my lord—

speaker: PRINCE
text: I will not hear you.

speaker: LEONATO
text: No? Come, brother, away. I will be heard.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: And shall, or some of us will smart for it.

speaker: PRINCE
text: See, see, here comes the man we went to seek.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Now, signior, what news?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Good day, my lord.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Welcome, signior. You are almost come to

speaker: PRINCE
text: part almost a fray.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: We had like to have had our two noses

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: snapped off with two old men without teeth.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Leonato and his brother. What think’st thou?

speaker: PRINCE
text: Had we fought, I doubt we should have been too

speaker: PRINCE
text: young for them.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: In a false quarrel there is no true valor. I

speaker: BENEDICK
text: came to seek you both.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: We have been up and down to seek thee, for

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: we are high-proof melancholy and would fain have

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: it beaten away. Wilt thou use thy wit?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: It is in my scabbard. Shall I draw it?

speaker: PRINCE
text: Dost thou wear thy wit by thy side?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Never any did so, though very many have

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: been beside their wit. I will bid thee draw, as we do

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: the minstrels: draw to pleasure us.

speaker: PRINCE
text: As I am an honest man, he looks pale.—Art

speaker: PRINCE
text: thou sick, or angry?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: What, courage, man! What

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: though care killed a cat? Thou hast mettle enough

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: in thee to kill care.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Sir, I shall meet your wit in the career, an

speaker: BENEDICK
text: you charge it against me. I pray you, choose another

speaker: BENEDICK
text: subject.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Nay, then, give him another staff.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: This last was broke ’cross.

speaker: PRINCE
text: By this light, he changes more and more. I

speaker: PRINCE
text: think he be angry indeed.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: If he be, he knows how to turn his girdle.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Shall I speak a word in your ear?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: God bless me from a challenge!

speaker: BENEDICK
text: You are a villain. I jest

speaker: BENEDICK
text: not. I will make it good how you dare, with what you

speaker: BENEDICK
text: dare, and when you dare. Do me right, or I will

speaker: BENEDICK
text: protest your cowardice. You have killed a sweet

speaker: BENEDICK
text: lady, and her death shall fall heavy on you. Let me

speaker: BENEDICK
text: hear from you.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Well, I will meet you, so I may have good

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: cheer.

speaker: PRINCE
text: What, a feast, a feast?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I’ faith, I thank him. He hath bid me to a

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: calf’s head and a capon, the which if I do not carve

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: most curiously, say my knife’s naught. Shall I not

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: find a woodcock too?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Sir, your wit ambles well; it goes easily.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I’ll tell thee how Beatrice praised thy wit the

speaker: PRINCE
text: other day. I said thou hadst a fine wit. “True,” said

speaker: PRINCE
text: she, “a fine little one.” “No,” said I, “a great wit.”

speaker: PRINCE
text: “Right,” says she, “a great gross one.” “Nay,” said I,

speaker: PRINCE
text: “a good wit.” “Just,” said she, “it hurts nobody.”

speaker: PRINCE
text: “Nay,” said I, “the gentleman is wise.” “Certain,”

speaker: PRINCE
text: said she, “a wise gentleman.” “Nay,” said I, “he

speaker: PRINCE
text: hath the tongues.” “That I believe,” said she, “for he

speaker: PRINCE
text: swore a thing to me on Monday night which he

speaker: PRINCE
text: forswore on Tuesday morning; there’s a double

speaker: PRINCE
text: tongue, there’s two tongues.” Thus did she an hour

speaker: PRINCE
text: together transshape thy particular virtues. Yet at

speaker: PRINCE
text: last she concluded with a sigh, thou wast the

speaker: PRINCE
text: proper’st man in Italy.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: For the which she wept heartily and said she

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: cared not.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Yea, that she did. But yet for all that, an if she

speaker: PRINCE
text: did not hate him deadly, she would love him

speaker: PRINCE
text: dearly. The old man’s daughter told us all.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: All, all. And, moreover, God saw him when

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: he was hid in the garden.

speaker: PRINCE
text: But when shall we set the savage bull’s horns

speaker: PRINCE
text: on the sensible Benedick’s head?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Yea, and text underneath: “Here dwells Benedick,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: the married man”?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Fare you well, boy. You know my mind. I

speaker: BENEDICK
text: will leave you now to your gossip-like humor. You

speaker: BENEDICK
text: break jests as braggarts do their blades, which, God

speaker: BENEDICK
text: be thanked, hurt not.—My lord, for your many

speaker: BENEDICK
text: courtesies I thank you. I must discontinue your

speaker: BENEDICK
text: company. Your brother the Bastard is fled from

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Messina. You have among you killed a sweet and

speaker: BENEDICK
text: innocent lady. For my Lord Lackbeard there, he and

speaker: BENEDICK
text: I shall meet, and till then peace be with him.

speaker: PRINCE
text: He is in earnest.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: In most profound earnest, and, I’ll warrant

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: you, for the love of Beatrice.

speaker: PRINCE
text: And hath challenged thee?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Most sincerely.

speaker: PRINCE
text: What a pretty thing man is when he goes in his

speaker: PRINCE
text: doublet and hose and leaves off his wit!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: He is then a giant to an ape; but then is an ape

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: a doctor to such a man.

speaker: PRINCE
text: But soft you, let me be. Pluck up, my heart,

speaker: PRINCE
text: and be sad. Did he not say my brother was fled?

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: Come you, sir. If justice cannot tame you,

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: she shall ne’er weigh more reasons in her balance.

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: Nay, an you be a cursing hypocrite once, you must

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: be looked to.

speaker: PRINCE
text: How now, two of my brother’s men bound?

speaker: PRINCE
text: Borachio one!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Hearken after their offense, my lord.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Officers, what offense have these men done?

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: Marry, sir, they have committed false

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: report; moreover, they have spoken untruths;

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: secondarily, they are slanders; sixth and lastly, they

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: have belied a lady; thirdly, they have verified unjust

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: things; and, to conclude, they are lying knaves.

speaker: PRINCE
text: First, I ask thee what they have done; thirdly, I

speaker: PRINCE
text: ask thee what’s their offense; sixth and lastly, why

speaker: PRINCE
text: they are committed; and, to conclude, what you lay

speaker: PRINCE
text: to their charge.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Rightly reasoned, and in his own division;

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: and, by my troth, there’s one meaning well suited.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Who have you offended,

speaker: PRINCE
text: masters, that you are thus bound to your

speaker: PRINCE
text: answer? This learned constable is too cunning to be

speaker: PRINCE
text: understood. What’s your offense?

speaker: BORACHIO
text: Sweet prince, let me go no farther to mine

speaker: BORACHIO
text: answer. Do you hear me, and let this count kill me.

speaker: BORACHIO
text: I have deceived even your very eyes. What your

speaker: BORACHIO
text: wisdoms could not discover, these shallow fools

speaker: BORACHIO
text: have brought to light, who in the night overheard

speaker: BORACHIO
text: me confessing to this man how Don John your

speaker: BORACHIO
text: brother incensed me to slander the Lady Hero, how

speaker: BORACHIO
text: you were brought into the orchard and saw me

speaker: BORACHIO
text: court Margaret in Hero’s garments, how you disgraced

speaker: BORACHIO
text: her when you should marry her. My villainy

speaker: BORACHIO
text: they have upon record, which I had rather seal with

speaker: BORACHIO
text: my death than repeat over to my shame. The lady is

speaker: BORACHIO
text: dead upon mine and my master’s false accusation.

speaker: BORACHIO
text: And, briefly, I desire nothing but the reward of a

speaker: BORACHIO
text: villain.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Runs not this speech like iron through your blood?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I have drunk poison whiles he uttered it.

speaker: PRINCE
text: But did my brother set thee on to this?

speaker: BORACHIO
text: Yea, and paid me richly for the practice of

speaker: BORACHIO
text: it.

speaker: PRINCE
text: He is composed and framed of treachery,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And fled he is upon this villainy.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Sweet Hero, now thy image doth appear

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: In the rare semblance that I loved it first.

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: Come, bring away the plaintiffs. By this

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: time our sexton hath reformed Signior Leonato of

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: the matter. And, masters, do not forget to specify,

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: when time and place shall serve, that I am an ass.

speaker: VERGES
text: Here, here comes Master Signior Leonato,

speaker: VERGES
text: and the Sexton too.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Which is the villain? Let me see his eyes,

speaker: LEONATO
text: That, when I note another man like him,

speaker: LEONATO
text: I may avoid him. Which of these is he?

speaker: BORACHIO
text: If you would know your wronger, look on me.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Art thou the slave that with thy breath hast killed

speaker: LEONATO
text: Mine innocent child?

speaker: BORACHIO
text: Yea, even I alone.

speaker: LEONATO
text: No, not so, villain, thou beliest thyself.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Here stand a pair of honorable men—

speaker: LEONATO
text: A third is fled—that had a hand in it.—

speaker: LEONATO
text: I thank you, princes, for my daughter’s death.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Record it with your high and worthy deeds.

speaker: LEONATO
text: ’Twas bravely done, if you bethink you of it.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I know not how to pray your patience,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Yet I must speak. Choose your revenge yourself.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Impose me to what penance your invention

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Can lay upon my sin. Yet sinned I not

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: But in mistaking.

speaker: PRINCE
text: By my soul, nor I,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And yet to satisfy this good old man

speaker: PRINCE
text: I would bend under any heavy weight

speaker: PRINCE
text: That he’ll enjoin me to.

speaker: LEONATO
text: I cannot bid you bid my daughter live—

speaker: LEONATO
text: That were impossible—but, I pray you both,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Possess the people in Messina here

speaker: LEONATO
text: How innocent she died. And if your love

speaker: LEONATO
text: Can labor aught in sad invention,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Hang her an epitaph upon her tomb

speaker: LEONATO
text: And sing it to her bones. Sing it tonight.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Tomorrow morning come you to my house,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And since you could not be my son-in-law,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Be yet my nephew. My brother hath a daughter,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Almost the copy of my child that’s dead,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And she alone is heir to both of us.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Give her the right you should have giv’n her cousin,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And so dies my revenge.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: O, noble sir!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Your overkindness doth wring tears from me.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I do embrace your offer and dispose

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: For henceforth of poor Claudio.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Tomorrow then I will expect your coming.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Tonight I take my leave. This naughty man

speaker: LEONATO
text: Shall face to face be brought to Margaret,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Who I believe was packed in all this wrong,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Hired to it by your brother.

speaker: BORACHIO
text: No, by my soul, she was not,

speaker: BORACHIO
text: Nor knew not what she did when she spoke to me,

speaker: BORACHIO
text: But always hath been just and virtuous

speaker: BORACHIO
text: In anything that I do know by her.

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: Moreover, sir, which indeed is

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: not under white and black, this plaintiff here, the

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: offender, did call me ass. I beseech you, let it be

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: remembered in his punishment. And also the watch

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: heard them talk of one Deformed. They say he

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: wears a key in his ear and a lock hanging by it and

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: borrows money in God’s name, the which he hath

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: used so long and never paid that now men grow

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: hardhearted and will lend nothing for God’s sake.

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: Pray you, examine him upon that point.

speaker: LEONATO
text: I thank thee for thy care and honest pains.

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: Your Worship speaks like a most thankful

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: and reverent youth, and I praise God for you.

speaker: LEONATO
text: There’s for thy pains.

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: God save the foundation.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Go, I discharge thee of thy prisoner, and I

speaker: LEONATO
text: thank thee.

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: I leave an arrant knave with your Worship,

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: which I beseech your Worship to correct

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: yourself, for the example of others. God keep your

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: Worship! I wish your Worship well. God restore you

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: to health. I humbly give you leave to depart, and if a

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: merry meeting may be wished, God prohibit it.—

speaker: DOGBERRY
text: Come, neighbor.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Until tomorrow morning, lords, farewell.

speaker:
LEONATO’S
               
              BROTHER
text: Farewell, my lords. We look for you tomorrow.

speaker: PRINCE
text: We will not fail.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Tonight I’ll mourn with Hero.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Bring you these fellows on.—We’ll talk with Margaret,

speaker: LEONATO
text: How her acquaintance grew with this lewd fellow.
